query:
You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.50 (commit: ce93a6db).

## Issue to Resolve

No spacing around colon in annotation
Spacing should not be allowed around the colon in annotation and annotation entries regardless of the annotation target

```
@file:JvmName("Foo") // allowed
@file :JvmName("Foo") // disallowed
@file: JvmName("Foo") // disallowed
@file : JvmName("Foo") // disallowed

@set:[Inject VisibleForTesting] // allowed
@set :[Inject VisibleForTesting] // disallowed
@set: [Inject VisibleForTesting] // disallowed
@set : [Inject VisibleForTesting] // disallowed
```

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/SpacingAroundColonRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.KtLintAssertThat.Companion.assertThatRule
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.LintViolation
  5→import org.junit.jupiter.api.Nested
  6→import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test
  7→
  8→class SpacingAroundColonRuleTest {
  9→    private val spacingAroundColonRuleAssertThat = assertThatRule { SpacingAroundColonRule() }
 10→
 11→    @Test
 12→    fun `Given some child class declarations`() {
 13→        val code =
 14→            """
 15→            class A:B
 16→            class A2 : B2
 17→            """.trimIndent()
 18→        val formattedCode =
 19→            """
 20→            class A : B
 21→            class A2 : B2
 22→            """.trimIndent()
 23→        spacingAroundColonRuleAssertThat(code)
 24→            .hasLintViolation(1, 8, "Missing spacing around \":\"")
 25→            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
 26→    }
 27→
 28→    @Test
 29→    fun `Given some interfaces`() {
 30→        val code =
 31→            """
 32→            interface D
 33→            interface C: D
 34→            interface C2 : D
 35→            """.trimIndent()
 36→        val formattedCode =
 37→            """
 38→            interface D
 39→            interface C : D
 40→            interface C2 : D
 41→            """.trimIndent()
 42→        spacingAroundColonRuleAssertThat(code)
 43→            .hasLintViolation(2, 12, "Missing spacing before \":\"")
 44→            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
 45→    }
 46→
 47→    @Test
 48→    fun `Given some variable declarations inside a function`() {
 49→        val code =
 50→            """
 51→            fun main() {
 52→                var x:Boolean
 53→                var y: Boolean
 54→            }
 55→            """.trimIndent()
 56→        val formattedCode =
 57→            """
 58→            fun main() {
 59→                var x: Boolean
 60→                var y: Boolean
 61→            }
 62→            """.trimIndent()
 63→        spacingAroundColonRuleAssertThat(code)
 64→            .hasLintViolation(2, 11, "Missing spacing after \":\"")
 65→            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
 66→    }
 67→
 68→    @Test
 69→    fun `Given some object creation as parameter value in function without a space before the colon`() {
 70→        val code =
 71→            """
 72→            interface Foo
 73→            fun main() {
 74→                bar(object: Foo {})
 75→            }
 76→            """.trimIndent()
 77→        val formattedCode =
 78→            """
 79→            interface Foo
 80→            fun main() {
 81→                bar(object : Foo {})
 82→            }
 83→            """.trimIndent()
 84→        spacingAroundColonRuleAssertThat(code)
 85→            .hasLintViolation(3, 15, "Missing spacing before \":\"")
 86→            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
 87→    }
 88→
 89→    @Test
 90→    fun `Given some annotation use-site targets`() {
 91→        // https://kotlinlang.org/docs/annotations.html#annotation-use-site-targets
 92→        val code =
 93→            """
 94→            @file:JvmName("Foo")
 95→            class Example(@field:Ann val foo: String, @get:Ann val bar: String)
 96→            class Example {
 97→                @set:[Inject VisibleForTesting]
 98→                public var collaborator: Collaborator
 99→            }
100→            fun @receiver:Fancy String.myExtension() { }
101→            """.trimIndent()
102→        spacingAroundColonRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
103→    }
104→
105→    @Test
106→    fun `Given a double colon operator then do not add spaces`() {
107→        val code =
108→            """
109→            fun main() {
110→                val x = Foo::class
111→            }
112→            """.trimIndent()
113→        spacingAroundColonRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
114→    }
115→
116→    @Test
117→    fun testLint4() {
118→        val code =
119→            """
120→            class A {
121→                @Deprecated("") @Throws(IOException::class, SecurityException::class)
122→                protected abstract fun <T> f(
123→                    @Nullable thing: String, things: List<T>): Runnable where T : Runnable, T: Closeable
124→            }
125→            """.trimIndent()
126→        val formattedCode =
127→            """
128→            class A {
129→                @Deprecated("") @Throws(IOException::class, SecurityException::class)
130→                protected abstract fun <T> f(
131→                    @Nullable thing: String, things: List<T>): Runnable where T : Runnable, T : Closeable
132→            }
133→            """.trimIndent()
134→        spacingAroundColonRuleAssertThat(code)
135→            .hasLintViolation(4, 82, "Missing spacing before \":\"")
136→            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
137→    }
138→
139→    @Test
140→    fun `Given some classes missing spaces around the colon before the super type`() {
141→        val code =
142→            """

 38→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 39→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.descriptors.annotations.AnnotationUseSiteTarget
 40→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtAnnotationEntry
 41→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.siblings
 42→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.utils.addToStdlib.ifTrue
 43→
 44→/**
 45→ * Ensures that annotation are wrapped to separate lines.
 46→ *
 47→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#annotation-formatting
 48→ *
 49→ * 1) Place annotations on separate lines before the declaration to which they are attached, and with the same indentation:
 50→ *
 51→ *    ```
 52→ *    @Target(AnnotationTarget.PROPERTY)
 53→ *    annotation class JsonExclude
 54→ *    ```
 55→ *
 56→ * 2) Annotations without arguments may be placed on the same line:
 57→ *
 58→ *    ```
 59→ *    @JsonExclude @JvmField
 60→ *    var x: String
 61→ *    ```
 62→ *
 63→ * 3) A single annotation without arguments may be placed on the same line as the corresponding declaration:
 64→ *
 65→ *    ```
 66→ *    @Test fun foo() { /*...*/ }
 67→ *    ```
 68→ *
 69→ * 4) File annotations are placed after the file comment (if any), before the package statement, and are separated from package with a blank
 70→ *    line (to emphasize the fact that they target the file and not the package).
 71→ *
 72→ * @see [AnnotationSpacingRule] for white space rules.
 73→ */
 74→public class AnnotationRule :
 75→    StandardRule(
 76→        id = "annotation",
 77→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 78→            setOf(
 79→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 80→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 81→            ),
 82→        visitorModifiers =
 83→            setOf(
 84→                RunAfterRule(
 85→                    ruleId = ENUM_WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 86→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 87→                ),
 88→            ),
 89→    ) {
 90→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 91→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 92→
 93→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 94→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
 95→        indentConfig =
 96→            IndentConfig(
 97→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 98→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 99→            )
100→    }
101→
102→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
103→        node: ASTNode,
104→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
105→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
106→    ) {
107→        when (node.elementType) {
108→            FILE_ANNOTATION_LIST -> {
109→                visitAnnotationList(node, emit, autoCorrect)
110→                visitFileAnnotationList(node, emit, autoCorrect)
111→            }
112→            ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION, MODIFIER_LIST -> {
113→                visitAnnotationList(node, emit, autoCorrect)
114→            }
115→            ANNOTATION -> {
116→                // Annotation array
117→                //     @[...]
118→                visitAnnotation(node, emit, autoCorrect)
119→            }
120→            ANNOTATION_ENTRY -> {
121→                visitAnnotationEntry(node, emit, autoCorrect)
122→            }
123→            TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> {
124→                visitTypeArgumentList(node, emit, autoCorrect)
125→            }
126→        }
127→    }
128→
129→    private fun visitAnnotationList(
130→        node: ASTNode,
131→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
132→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
133→    ) {
134→        require(node.elementType in ANNOTATION_CONTAINER)
135→
136→        if (node.shouldWrapAnnotations()) {
137→            val expectedIndent =
138→                when {
139→                    node.elementType == ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION ->
140→                        indentConfig.siblingIndentOf(node)
141→                    node.hasAnnotationBeforeConstructor() ->
142→                        indentConfig.siblingIndentOf(node.treeParent)
143→                    else ->
144→                        indentConfig.parentIndentOf(node)
145→                }
146→
147→            node
148→                .children()
149→                .filter { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
150→                .filter {
151→                    it.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() ||
152→                        !it.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryWithoutParametersOnTheSameLine()
153→                }.forEach { annotationEntry ->
154→                    annotationEntry
155→                        .prevLeaf()
156→                        ?.let { prevLeaf ->
157→                            // Let the indentation rule determine the exact indentation and only report and fix when the line needs to be
158→                            // wrapped
159→                            if (!prevLeaf.textContains('\n')) {
160→                                emit(prevLeaf.startOffset, "Expected newline before annotation", true)
161→                                if (autoCorrect) {
162→                                    prevLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
163→                                        prevLeaf
164→                                            .text
165→                                            .substringBeforeLast('\n', "")
166→                                            .plus(expectedIndent),
167→                                    )
168→                                }
169→                            }
170→                        }
171→                }
172→
173→            node
174→                .children()
175→                .last { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
176→                .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
177→                .nextCodeLeaf()
178→                ?.prevLeaf()
179→                ?.let { prevLeaf ->
180→                    // Let the indentation rule determine the exact indentation and only report and fix when the line needs to be wrapped
181→                    if (!prevLeaf.textContains('\n')) {
182→                        emit(prevLeaf.startOffset, "Expected newline after last annotation", true)
183→                        if (autoCorrect) {
184→                            prevLeaf.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(expectedIndent)
185→                        }
186→                    }
187→                }
188→
189→            node
190→                .takeIf { it.elementType == ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION }
191→                ?.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
192→                ?.nextCodeLeaf()
193→                ?.prevLeaf()
194→                ?.let { leaf ->
195→                    // Let the indentation rule determine the exact indentation and only report and fix when the line needs to be wrapped
196→                    if (!leaf.textContains('\n')) {
197→                        emit(leaf.startOffset, "Expected newline", true)
198→                        if (autoCorrect) {
199→                            leaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(node.indent())
200→                        }
201→                    }
202→                }
203→        }
204→    }
205→
206→    private fun ASTNode.shouldWrapAnnotations() =
207→        hasAnnotationWithParameter() ||
208→            hasMultipleAnnotationsOnSameLine() ||
209→            hasAnnotationBeforeConstructor()
210→
211→    private fun ASTNode.hasAnnotationWithParameter(): Boolean {
212→        require(elementType in ANNOTATION_CONTAINER)
213→        return children()
214→            .any {
215→                it.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() &&
216→                    it.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
217→                    it.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
218→                    it.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation()
219→            }
220→    }
221→
222→    private fun ASTNode.hasMultipleAnnotationsOnSameLine(): Boolean {
223→        require(elementType in ANNOTATION_CONTAINER)
224→        return children()
225→            .any {
226→                it.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
227→                    it.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
228→                    it.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
229→                    it.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
230→                    it.isLastAnnotationEntry()
231→                // Code below is disallowed
232→                //   @Foo1 @Foo2 fun foo() {}
233→                // But following is allowed:
234→                //   @[Foo1 Foo2] fun foo() {}
235→                //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
236→            }
237→    }
238→
239→    private fun ASTNode.hasAnnotationBeforeConstructor() =
240→        codeStyle == CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official &&
241→            hasAnnotationEntry() &&
242→            nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CONSTRUCTOR_KEYWORD
243→
244→    private fun ASTNode.hasAnnotationEntry() = children().any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
245→
246→    private fun visitTypeArgumentList(
247→        node: ASTNode,
248→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
249→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
250→    ) {
251→        node
252→            .children()
253→            .filter { it.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION }
254→            .mapNotNull { it.findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE) }
255→            .filter { it.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE }
256→            .mapNotNull { it.findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST) }
257→            .filter { it.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST }
258→            .any { it.shouldWrapAnnotations() }
259→            .ifTrue { wrapTypeArgumentList(node, emit, autoCorrect) }
260→    }
261→
262→    private fun wrapTypeArgumentList(
263→        node: ASTNode,
264→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
265→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
266→    ) {
267→        val expectedIndent = indentConfig.childIndentOf(node)
268→
269→        node
270→            .children()
271→            .filter { it.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION }
272→            .forEach { typeProjection ->
273→                typeProjection
274→                    .prevLeaf()
275→                    ?.let { prevLeaf ->
276→                        if (prevLeaf.text != expectedIndent) {
277→                            emit(prevLeaf.startOffset, "Expected newline", true)
278→                            if (autoCorrect) {
279→                                prevLeaf.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(expectedIndent)
280→                            }
281→                        }
282→                    }
283→            }
284→
285→        node
286→            .findChildByType(GT)
287→            ?.let { gt ->
288→                if (gt.prevLeaf()?.text != expectedIndent) {
289→                    emit(gt.startOffset, "Expected newline", true)
290→                    if (autoCorrect) {
291→                        gt.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expectedIndent)
292→                    }
293→                }
294→            }
295→    }
296→
297→    private fun visitAnnotationEntry(
298→        node: ASTNode,
299→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
300→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
301→    ) {
302→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
303→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() && node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {
304→            // Code below is disallowed
305→            //   @Foo1
306→            //   @Foo2 @Foo3
307→            //   fun foo() {}
308→            emit(
309→                node.startOffset,
310→                "All annotations should either be on a single line or all annotations should be on a separate line",
311→                true,
312→            )
313→            if (autoCorrect) {
314→                node
315→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
316→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
317→            }
318→        }
319→    }
320→
321→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine(): Boolean {
322→        val firstAnnotation = treeParent.findChildByType(ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
323→        return siblings(forward = false)
324→            .takeWhile { it != firstAnnotation }
325→            .any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
326→    }
327→
328→    private fun ASTNode.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation() = getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() != AnnotationUseSiteTarget.RECEIVER
329→
330→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() =
331→        psi
332→            .safeAs<KtAnnotationEntry>()
333→            ?.useSiteTarget
334→            ?.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget()
335→
336→    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() = getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() != null
337→
338→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() =
339→        takeIf { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
340→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST)
341→
342→    private fun ASTNode.isLastAnnotationEntry() =
343→        this ==
344→            treeParent
345→                .children()
346→                .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
347→
348→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryWithoutParametersOnTheSameLine() =
349→        siblings(forward = false)
350→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() && !it.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() }
351→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY && !it.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() }
352→
353→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() =
354→        siblings(forward = false)
355→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
356→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
357→
358→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() =
359→        siblings(forward = false)
360→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
361→
362→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry() =
363→        siblings(forward = false)
364→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
365→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
366→
367→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() = siblings(forward = true).any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
368→
369→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry() =
370→        siblings(forward = true)
371→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
372→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
373→
374→    private fun visitFileAnnotationList(
375→        node: ASTNode,
376→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
377→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
378→    ) {
379→        node
380→            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
381→            .nextCodeLeaf()
382→            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
383→                val whitespaceBefore = codeLeaf.prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
384→
385→                if (whitespaceBefore == null || whitespaceBefore.text != "\n\n") {
386→                    emit(
387→                        codeLeaf.startOffset,
388→                        "File annotations should be separated from file contents with a blank line",
389→                        true,
390→                    )
391→                    if (autoCorrect) {
392→                        codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n\n")
393→                    }
394→                }
395→            }
396→    }
397→
398→    private fun visitAnnotation(
399→        node: ASTNode,
400→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
401→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
402→    ) {
403→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION)
404→
405→        if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
406→            (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry())
407→        ) {
408→            emit(
409→                node.startOffset,
410→                "@[...] style annotations should be on a separate line from other annotations.",
411→                true,
412→            )
413→            if (autoCorrect) {
414→                if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
415→                    node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
416→                } else if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
417→                    node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
418→                }
419→            }
420→        }
421→    }
422→
423→    private fun getNewlineWithIndent(modifierListRoot: ASTNode): String {
424→        val nodeBeforeAnnotations =
425→            modifierListRoot
426→                .treeParent
427→                .treePrev
428→        // Make sure we only insert a single newline
429→        val indentWithoutNewline =
430→            nodeBeforeAnnotations
431→                ?.text
432→                .orEmpty()
433→                .substringAfterLast('\n')
434→        return "\n".plus(indentWithoutNewline)
435→    }
436→
437→    private companion object {
438→        val ANNOTATION_CONTAINER =
439→            listOf(
440→                ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION,
441→                FILE_ANNOTATION_LIST,
442→                MODIFIER_LIST,
443→            )
444→    }
445→}
446→
447→public val ANNOTATION_RULE_ID: RuleId = AnnotationRule().ruleId
448→

conversation_history.content:
1→# Changelog
 2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
 3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
 4→
 5→## Unreleased
 6→
 7→### Added
 8→
 9→### Removed
10→
11→### Fixed
12→
13→* Allow to disable ktlint in `.editorconfig` for a glob ([#2100](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2100))
14→* Fix wrapping of nested function literals `wrapping` ([#2106](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2106))
15→* Do not indent class body for classes having a long super type list in code style `ktlint_official` as it is inconsistent compared to other class bodies `indent` [#2115](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2115) 
16→
17→### Changed
18→
19→* Update dependency gradle to v8.2.1 ([#2122](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2122))
20→* Update dependency org.codehaus.janino:janino to v3.1.10  ([#2110](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2110))
21→* Update dependency com.google.jimfs:jimfs to v1.3.0 ([#2112](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2112))
22→
23→## [0.50.0] - 2023-06-29
24→
25→### Deprecation of ktlint-enable and ktlint-disable directives
26→
27→The `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives are no longer supported. Ktlint rules can now only be suppressed using the `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations. A new rule, `internal:ktlint-suppression`, is provided to replace the directives with annotations.
28→
29→API consumers do not need to provide this rule, but it does no harm when done.
30→
31→The `internal:ktlint-suppression` rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` nor via `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations.
32→
33→### Custom Rule Providers need to prepare for Kotlin 1.9
34→
35→In Kotlin 1.9 the extension points of the embedded kotlin compiler will change. Ktlint only uses the `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` extension point. This extension is not yet supported in 1.9, neither is it documented ([#KT-58704](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-58704/Support-and-document-extension-point-org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler)). Without this extension point it might happen that your custom rules will throw exceptions during runtime. See [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981).
36→
37→In Ktlint, 7 out of 77 rules needed small and sometimes bigger changes to become independent of the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler`. The impact on your custom rules may vary dependent on the way the autocorrect has been implemented. When manipulating `ASTNode`s there seems to be no impact. When, manipulating `PsiElement`s, some functions consistently result in a runtime exception.
38→
39→Based on the refactoring of the rules as provided by `ktlint-ruleset-standard` in Ktlint `0.49.x` the suggested refactoring is as follows:
40→
41→* Replace `LeafElement.replaceWithText(String)` with `LeafElement.rawReplaceWithText(String)`.
42→* Replace `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)` with `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)`. Note that this method inserts the new node (first) argument *before* the second argument node and as of that is not a simple replacement of the `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)`.
43→* Replace `PsiElement.replace(PsiElement)` with a sequence of `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)` and `AstNode.removeChild(AstNode)`.
44→
45→Be aware that your custom rules might use other functions which also throw exceptions when the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is no longer supported.
46→
47→In order to help you to analyse and fix the problems with your custom rules, ktlint temporarily supports to disable the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` using a flag. This flag is available in the Ktlint CLI and in the `KtlintRuleEngine`. By default, the extension point is enabled like it was in previous versions of ktlint.
48→
49→At least you should analyse the problems by running your test suits by running ktlint and disabling the extension point. Next you can start with fixing and releasing the updated rules. All rules in this version of ktlint have already been refactored and are not dependent on the extension point anymore. In Ktlint CLI the flag is to be activated with parameter `--disable-kotlin-extension-point`. API Consumers that use the `KtlintRuleEngine` directly, have to set property `enableKotlinCompilerExtensionPoint` to `false`.
50→

content.text:
I have resolved the issue where spacing was allowed around the colon in annotation use-site targets.

### Changes made:
- Modified `SpacingAroundColonRule.kt` to explicitly handle colons that are direct children of `ANNOTATION` or `ANNOTATION_ENTRY` elements (which correspond to annotation use-site targets).
- Enforced no spacing or newlines before the colon, and no spacing after the colon for these cases.
- Removed the previous early return that was skipping all colons inside annotations, which was also causing colons in other contexts (like object literals inside annotation arguments) to be ignored.
- Updated `CHANGELOG.md` to document the fix.
- Created a new test file `SpacingAroundColonAnnotationRuleTest.kt` with comprehensive test cases to verify the fix and ensure no regressions for other colons inside annotations.

### Verification:
- Ran the new test cases, which all pass.
- Ran all existing tests in `ktlint-ruleset-standard`, which also pass, ensuring no regressions.

```kotlin
// Example of fixed behavior:
@file:JvmName("Foo") // allowed
@file :JvmName("Foo") // reported as "Unexpected spacing before ":"" and auto-corrected
@file: JvmName("Foo") // reported as "Unexpected spacing after ":"" and auto-corrected
@file
:JvmName("Foo") // reported as "Unexpected newline before ":"" and auto-corrected
```

 27→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.indent

 37→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 38→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 39→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.descriptors.annotations.AnnotationUseSiteTarget
 40→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtAnnotationEntry
 41→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.siblings
 42→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.utils.addToStdlib.ifTrue
 43→
 44→/**
 45→ * Ensures that annotation are wrapped to separate lines.
 46→ *
 47→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#annotation-formatting
 48→ *
 49→ * 1) Place annotations on separate lines before the declaration to which they are attached, and with the same indentation:
 50→ *
 51→ *    ```
 52→ *    @Target(AnnotationTarget.PROPERTY)
 53→ *    annotation class JsonExclude
 54→ *    ```
 55→ *
 56→ * 2) Annotations without arguments may be placed on the same line:
 57→ *
 58→ *    ```
 59→ *    @JsonExclude @JvmField
 60→ *    var x: String
 61→ *    ```
 62→ *
 63→ * 3) A single annotation without arguments may be placed on the same line as the corresponding declaration:
 64→ *
 65→ *    ```
 66→ *    @Test fun foo() { /*...*/ }
 67→ *    ```
 68→ *
 69→ * 4) File annotations are placed after the file comment (if any), before the package statement, and are separated from package with a blank
 70→ *    line (to emphasize the fact that they target the file and not the package).
 71→ *
 72→ * @see [AnnotationSpacingRule] for white space rules.
 73→ */
 74→public class AnnotationRule :
 75→    StandardRule(
 76→        id = "annotation",
 77→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 78→            setOf(
 79→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 80→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 81→            ),
 82→        visitorModifiers =
 83→            setOf(
 84→                RunAfterRule(
 85→                    ruleId = ENUM_WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 86→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 87→                ),
 88→            ),
 89→    ) {
 90→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 91→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 92→
 93→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 94→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
 95→        indentConfig =
 96→            IndentConfig(
 97→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 98→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 99→            )
100→    }
101→
102→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
103→        node: ASTNode,
104→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
105→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
106→    ) {
107→        when (node.elementType) {
108→            FILE_ANNOTATION_LIST -> {
109→                visitAnnotationList(node, emit, autoCorrect)
110→                visitFileAnnotationList(node, emit, autoCorrect)
111→            }
112→            ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION, MODIFIER_LIST -> {
113→                visitAnnotationList(node, emit, autoCorrect)
114→            }
115→            ANNOTATION -> {
116→                // Annotation array
117→                //     @[...]
118→                visitAnnotation(node, emit, autoCorrect)
119→            }
120→            ANNOTATION_ENTRY -> {
121→                visitAnnotationEntry(node, emit, autoCorrect)
122→            }
123→            TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> {
124→                visitTypeArgumentList(node, emit, autoCorrect)
125→            }
126→        }
127→    }
128→
129→    private fun visitAnnotationList(
130→        node: ASTNode,
131→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
132→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
133→    ) {
134→        require(node.elementType in ANNOTATION_CONTAINER)
135→
136→        if (node.shouldWrapAnnotations()) {
137→            val expectedIndent =
138→                when {
139→                    node.elementType == ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION ->
140→                        indentConfig.siblingIndentOf(node)
141→                    node.hasAnnotationBeforeConstructor() ->
142→                        indentConfig.siblingIndentOf(node.treeParent)
143→                    else ->
144→                        indentConfig.parentIndentOf(node)
145→                }
146→
147→            node
148→                .children()
149→                .filter { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
150→                .filter {
151→                    it.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() ||
152→                        !it.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryWithoutParametersOnTheSameLine()
153→                }.forEach { annotationEntry ->
154→                    annotationEntry
155→                        .prevLeaf()
156→                        ?.let { prevLeaf ->
157→                            // Let the indentation rule determine the exact indentation and only report and fix when the line needs to be
158→                            // wrapped
159→                            if (!prevLeaf.textContains('\n')) {
160→                                emit(prevLeaf.startOffset, "Expected newline before annotation", true)
161→                                if (autoCorrect) {
162→                                    prevLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
163→                                        prevLeaf
164→                                            .text
165→                                            .substringBeforeLast('\n', "")
166→                                            .plus(expectedIndent),
167→                                    )
168→                                }
169→                            }
170→                        }
171→                }
172→
173→            node
174→                .children()
175→                .last { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
176→                .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
177→                .nextCodeLeaf()
178→                ?.prevLeaf()
179→                ?.let { prevLeaf ->
180→                    // Let the indentation rule determine the exact indentation and only report and fix when the line needs to be wrapped
181→                    if (!prevLeaf.textContains('\n')) {
182→                        emit(prevLeaf.startOffset, "Expected newline after last annotation", true)
183→                        if (autoCorrect) {
184→                            prevLeaf.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(expectedIndent)
185→                        }
186→                    }
187→                }
188→
189→            node
190→                .takeIf { it.elementType == ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION }
191→                ?.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
192→                ?.nextCodeLeaf()
193→                ?.prevLeaf()
194→                ?.let { leaf ->
195→                    // Let the indentation rule determine the exact indentation and only report and fix when the line needs to be wrapped
196→                    if (!leaf.textContains('\n')) {
197→                        emit(leaf.startOffset, "Expected newline", true)
198→                        if (autoCorrect) {
199→                            leaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(node.indent())
200→                        }
201→                    }
202→                }
203→        }
204→    }
205→
206→    private fun ASTNode.shouldWrapAnnotations() =
207→        hasAnnotationWithParameter() ||
208→            hasMultipleAnnotationsOnSameLine() ||
209→            hasAnnotationBeforeConstructor()
210→
211→    private fun ASTNode.hasAnnotationWithParameter(): Boolean {
212→        require(elementType in ANNOTATION_CONTAINER)
213→        return children()
214→            .any {
215→                it.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() &&
216→                    it.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
217→                    it.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
218→                    it.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation()
219→            }
220→    }
221→
222→    private fun ASTNode.hasMultipleAnnotationsOnSameLine(): Boolean {
223→        require(elementType in ANNOTATION_CONTAINER)
224→        return children()
225→            .any {
226→                it.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
227→                    it.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
228→                    it.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
229→                    it.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
230→                    it.isLastAnnotationEntry()
231→                // Code below is disallowed
232→                //   @Foo1 @Foo2 fun foo() {}
233→                // But following is allowed:
234→                //   @[Foo1 Foo2] fun foo() {}
235→                //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
236→            }
237→    }
238→
239→    private fun ASTNode.hasAnnotationBeforeConstructor() =
240→        codeStyle == CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official &&
241→            hasAnnotationEntry() &&
242→            nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CONSTRUCTOR_KEYWORD
243→
244→    private fun ASTNode.hasAnnotationEntry() = children().any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
245→
246→    private fun visitTypeArgumentList(
247→        node: ASTNode,
248→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
249→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
250→    ) {
251→        node
252→            .children()
253→            .filter { it.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION }
254→            .mapNotNull { it.findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE) }
255→            .filter { it.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE }
256→            .mapNotNull { it.findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST) }
257→            .filter { it.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST }
258→            .any { it.shouldWrapAnnotations() }
259→            .ifTrue { wrapTypeArgumentList(node, emit, autoCorrect) }
260→    }
261→
262→    private fun wrapTypeArgumentList(
263→        node: ASTNode,
264→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
265→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
266→    ) {
267→        val expectedIndent = indentConfig.childIndentOf(node)
268→
269→        node
270→            .children()
271→            .filter { it.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION }
272→            .forEach { typeProjection ->
273→                typeProjection
274→                    .prevLeaf()
275→                    ?.let { prevLeaf ->
276→                        if (prevLeaf.text != expectedIndent) {
277→                            emit(prevLeaf.startOffset, "Expected newline", true)
278→                            if (autoCorrect) {
279→                                prevLeaf.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(expectedIndent)
280→                            }
281→                        }
282→                    }
283→            }
284→
285→        node
286→            .findChildByType(GT)
287→            ?.let { gt ->
288→                if (gt.prevLeaf()?.text != expectedIndent) {
289→                    emit(gt.startOffset, "Expected newline", true)
290→                    if (autoCorrect) {
291→                        gt.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expectedIndent)
292→                    }
293→                }
294→            }
295→    }
296→
297→    private fun visitAnnotationEntry(
298→        node: ASTNode,
299→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
300→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
301→    ) {
302→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
303→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() && node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {
304→            // Code below is disallowed
305→            //   @Foo1
306→            //   @Foo2 @Foo3
307→            //   fun foo() {}
308→            emit(
309→                node.startOffset,
310→                "All annotations should either be on a single line or all annotations should be on a separate line",
311→                true,
312→            )
313→            if (autoCorrect) {
314→                node
315→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
316→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
317→            }
318→        }
319→    }
320→
321→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine(): Boolean {
322→        val firstAnnotation = treeParent.findChildByType(ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
323→        return siblings(forward = false)
324→            .takeWhile { it != firstAnnotation }
325→            .any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
326→    }
327→
328→    private fun ASTNode.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation() = getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() != AnnotationUseSiteTarget.RECEIVER
329→
330→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() =
331→        psi
332→            .safeAs<KtAnnotationEntry>()
333→            ?.useSiteTarget
334→            ?.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget()
335→
336→    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() = getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() != null
337→
338→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() =
339→        takeIf { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
340→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST)
341→
342→    private fun ASTNode.isLastAnnotationEntry() =
343→        this ==
344→            treeParent
345→                .children()
346→                .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
347→
348→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryWithoutParametersOnTheSameLine() =
349→        siblings(forward = false)
350→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() && !it.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() }
351→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY && !it.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() }
352→
353→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() =
354→        siblings(forward = false)
355→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
356→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
357→
358→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() =
359→        siblings(forward = false)
360→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
361→
362→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry() =
363→        siblings(forward = false)
364→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
365→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
366→
367→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() = siblings(forward = true).any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
368→
369→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry() =
370→        siblings(forward = true)
371→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
372→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
373→
374→    private fun visitFileAnnotationList(
375→        node: ASTNode,
376→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
377→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
378→    ) {
379→        node
380→            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
381→            .nextCodeLeaf()
382→            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
383→                val whitespaceBefore = codeLeaf.prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
384→
385→                if (whitespaceBefore == null || whitespaceBefore.text != "\n\n") {
386→                    emit(
387→                        codeLeaf.startOffset,
388→                        "File annotations should be separated from file contents with a blank line",
389→                        true,
390→                    )
391→                    if (autoCorrect) {
392→                        codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n\n")
393→                    }
394→                }
395→            }
396→    }
397→
398→    private fun visitAnnotation(
399→        node: ASTNode,
400→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
401→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
402→    ) {
403→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION)
404→
405→        if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
406→            (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry())
407→        ) {
408→            emit(
409→                node.startOffset,
410→                "@[...] style annotations should be on a separate line from other annotations.",
411→                true,
412→            )
413→            if (autoCorrect) {
414→                if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
415→                    node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
416→                } else if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
417→                    node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
418→                }
419→            }
420→        }
421→    }
422→
423→    private fun getNewlineWithIndent(modifierListRoot: ASTNode): String {
424→        val nodeBeforeAnnotations =
425→            modifierListRoot
426→                .treeParent
427→                .treePrev
428→        // Make sure we only insert a single newline
429→        val indentWithoutNewline =
430→            nodeBeforeAnnotations
431→                ?.text
432→                .orEmpty()
433→                .substringAfterLast('\n')
434→        return "\n".plus(indentWithoutNewline)
435→    }
436→
437→    private companion object {
438→        val ANNOTATION_CONTAINER =
439→            listOf(
440→                ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION,
441→                FILE_ANNOTATION_LIST,
442→                MODIFIER_LIST,
443→            )
444→    }
445→}
446→
447→public val ANNOTATION_RULE_ID: RuleId = AnnotationRule().ruleId
448→

1→# Changelog
 2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
 3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
 4→
 5→## Unreleased
 6→
 7→### Added
 8→
 9→### Removed
10→
11→### Fixed
12→
13→* Allow to disable ktlint in `.editorconfig` for a glob ([#2100](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2100))
14→* Fix wrapping of nested function literals `wrapping` ([#2106](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2106))
15→* Do not indent class body for classes having a long super type list in code style `ktlint_official` as it is inconsistent compared to other class bodies `indent` [#2115](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2115) 
16→
17→### Changed
18→
19→* Update dependency gradle to v8.2.1 ([#2122](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2122))
20→* Update dependency org.codehaus.janino:janino to v3.1.10  ([#2110](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2110))
21→* Update dependency com.google.jimfs:jimfs to v1.3.0 ([#2112](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2112))
22→
23→## [0.50.0] - 2023-06-29
24→
25→### Deprecation of ktlint-enable and ktlint-disable directives
26→
27→The `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives are no longer supported. Ktlint rules can now only be suppressed using the `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations. A new rule, `internal:ktlint-suppression`, is provided to replace the directives with annotations.
28→
29→API consumers do not need to provide this rule, but it does no harm when done.
30→
31→The `internal:ktlint-suppression` rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` nor via `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations.
32→
33→### Custom Rule Providers need to prepare for Kotlin 1.9
34→
35→In Kotlin 1.9 the extension points of the embedded kotlin compiler will change. Ktlint only uses the `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` extension point. This extension is not yet supported in 1.9, neither is it documented ([#KT-58704](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-58704/Support-and-document-extension-point-org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler)). Without this extension point it might happen that your custom rules will throw exceptions during runtime. See [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981).
36→
37→In Ktlint, 7 out of 77 rules needed small and sometimes bigger changes to become independent of the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler`. The impact on your custom rules may vary dependent on the way the autocorrect has been implemented. When manipulating `ASTNode`s there seems to be no impact. When, manipulating `PsiElement`s, some functions consistently result in a runtime exception.
38→
39→Based on the refactoring of the rules as provided by `ktlint-ruleset-standard` in Ktlint `0.49.x` the suggested refactoring is as follows:
40→
41→* Replace `LeafElement.replaceWithText(String)` with `LeafElement.rawReplaceWithText(String)`.
42→* Replace `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)` with `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)`. Note that this method inserts the new node (first) argument *before* the second argument node and as of that is not a simple replacement of the `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)`.
43→* Replace `PsiElement.replace(PsiElement)` with a sequence of `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)` and `AstNode.removeChild(AstNode)`.
44→
45→Be aware that your custom rules might use other functions which also throw exceptions when the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is no longer supported.
46→
47→In order to help you to analyse and fix the problems with your custom rules, ktlint temporarily supports to disable the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` using a flag. This flag is available in the Ktlint CLI and in the `KtlintRuleEngine`. By default, the extension point is enabled like it was in previous versions of ktlint.
48→
49→At least you should analyse the problems by running your test suits by running ktlint and disabling the extension point. Next you can start with fixing and releasing the updated rules. All rules in this version of ktlint have already been refactored and are not dependent on the extension point anymore. In Ktlint CLI the flag is to be activated with parameter `--disable-kotlin-extension-point`. API Consumers that use the `KtlintRuleEngine` directly, have to set property `enableKotlinCompilerExtensionPoint` to `false`.
50→
